An act of sabotage grinds London to a halt. Taverner interrogates Roddy. Coe is convinced a destabilization strategy is at play.
Flyte sets out to find Roddy's girlfriend. The gang is dispatched to two different campaign events to prevent another attack.
Lamb debriefs River and Coe. Roddy is pulled in to help decipher a piece of code as the destabilization strategy nears its final stage.
It's up to the Slow Horses to stop Farouk's team from completing its mission in a final act of mass carnage.

Pryce Cahill was headed for golf greatness when an on-course meltdown derailed his career. Now struggling to stay afloat, he goes all in to mentor Santi—a teenage phenom with immense potential—and maybe save himself.
Pryce is down on his luck when a chance encounter at the driving range sends him on a mission to secure the opportunity of a lifetime.
Mitts and Pryce evaluate Santi's game over 18 holes. While testing out new clubs, Pryce tries to impart some wisdom about staying in the moment.
The gang hits the road in Mitts' RV. Pryce's coaching methods get tested in their first tourney. Santi makes a friend who unlocks his game.
Pryce does everything he can to break through to Santi and get back to training. Zero is presented with an unexpected offer.
Santi tees off at the qualifier with a new caddy as Pryce deploys covert coaching tactics. Mitts delivers harsh truths—and gets some right back.
Ahead of the U.S. Amateur Championship, Pryce gives everyone a rest day on the campgrounds. Mitts takes a risk. Santi makes a painful discovery.
After a blowup with Santi, Pryce is lost in thought about his past and what could've been. The gang prepare to go their separate ways.
A brazen plan is hatched to hustle Clark Ross. Pryce gets a chance to prove he was always the better golfer.
Pryce tries to help Santi deal with the pressure of his first PGA tournament. Romantic sparks fly as Santi starts climbing the leaderboard.
On the last day of the tournament, an unexpected visitor shakes things up while Santi's golf dreams hang in the balance.
An enigmatic private detective struggles with personal demons as he investigates the disappearance of a Hollywood producer's beloved granddaughter.
After solving a case in Japan, private eye John Sugar returns to LA to help find a missing woman with ties to a Hollywood legend.
Sugar suspects Melanie's not telling the full story. Meanwhile, he and Ruby may not be the only ones looking into the mysterious body in Olivia's trunk.
Melanie and Sugar get caught in a dicey situation—and make a new enemy. Sugar reunites with old friends, but he senses some are keeping secrets.
Sugar and Melanie track down Olivia's friend, who shares disturbing information about Davy and his family. Davy and Kenny dig into Sugar's past.
Melanie gets a surprise visit that unleashes Sugar's dark side. Ruby receives chilling guidance from a colleague. Davy makes a tragic decision.
Sugar thinks he's made a break in the case. When a double cross forces him to do the unthinkable, he turns to Melanie and Henry for help.
Sugar's fears for the safety of his friends lead him to a clandestine gathering where he learns a dangerous secret—and the truth about Olivia.
A shocking realization points Sugar toward a discovery that changes everything.

Season two ushers in the return of Los Angeles' iconic private detective and film connoisseur, John Sugar, in a new case, tracking the troubled older brother of an up-and-coming local boxer, as his search for his beloved missing sister continues. As the investigation expands into a citywide conspiracy with sinister intentions, Sugar must reckon with himself to answer the question — how far will he go to do what's right?

Tamar is a Mossad hacker-agent who infiltrates Tehran under a false identity to help destroy Iran's nuclear reactor. But when her mission fails, Tamar must plan an operation that will place everyone dear to her in jeopardy.
Tamar Rabinyan, a gifted hacker and a Mossad agent, enters the heart of Tehran to neutralize army radar systems and enable Israel's air force to bomb Iran's nuclear reactor.
Tabrizi, a local Mossad agent, tries to help Tamar in any way possible, while Revolutionary Guard officer Faraz is hot on Tamar's trail.
Hiding with her aunt, Tamar goes underground and attempts to escape Iran. Faraz and Tabrizi broaden their searches for her.
When she finds a commune of young regime opponents, Tamar takes refuge with them—and becomes close with Milad.
At an underground rave with Milad and the other commune members, Tamar must prove her loyalty. Faraz and Tabrizi reach a boiling point.
Tamar and Milad return to Tehran and blackmail their electric company contact. Kadosh tries to convince Gorev to approve Tamar's new plan.
The incident at the commune leaves Tamar shattered. Kadosh tries to help her recover so they can set the power plant operation in motion.

episodes.name: Five Hours Until the Bombing Run

info.plot: With Israeli planes approaching the reactor, Tamar, Milad, and Kadosh launch an operation as Faraz closes in on them.
Free of the Mossad and preparing to start a new life in Canada, Tamar is drawn back in by an irresistible offer.
A distraught Tamar chooses to stay in Tehran and seek justice—but learns the Mossad has put Marjan Montazemi in charge.
Determined to neutralize Faraz, Marjan makes a risky move. Tamar finds a way to get closer to Mohammadi. Milad begins behaving irresponsibly.
Tamar attends an elite pool party to target Peyman. Marjan reconsiders the orders she's been given about Faraz.
When Milad is arrested by Tehran police, Tamar and Faraz each race to reach him. Going against orders, Tamar continues pursuing Mohammadi.
In a dire situation, Faraz makes a difficult decision. Tamar and Peyman continue getting closer as Milad begins to question her priorities.
Peyman tells Tamar about his father's car race, leading the Mossad to devise a plan to take out Mohammadi once and for all.
Tamar doesn't know who to trust. A cat-and-mouse game leads to deadly consequences.
Fleeing pressure from her own side, Tamar hides out before making a vital discovery. Faraz and Nahid consider what to do next.
Tamar calls on an old enemy for help before meeting her match. Faraz feels the squeeze. Peterson faces disaster.
Finding herself in a tight spot, Tamar uses it to her advantage. Nahid makes a pact. Peterson turns up where he shouldn't be.
The fallout continues as Peterson's situation worsens. Nahid becomes more desperate as Tamar is forced closer to Ramin.
Faraz makes a surprising offer. Tamar and Nissan contemplate what lies ahead.
Tamar and Nissan regroup after a shocking setback. Faraz's prospects improve but Nahid feels the walls close in. Peterson gets to work.
As Peterson gets closer to his goal, Tamar recruits an unlikely ally. Nahid does what she must. Nissan confronts his past.
Peterson approaches his endgame. Grappling with difficult choices and stark consequences, Tamar and Faraz face off.
The mayor of a New England town resolves to turn it into the next tourist hot spot, despite local warnings that it's cursed.

Widow's Bay is a quaint island town 40 miles off the coast of New England. But something lurks beneath the surface. Mayor Tom Loftis is desperate to revive his struggling community. There's no Wi-Fi, spotty cellular reception and he must contend with superstitious locals who believe their island is cursed. He wants these people to respect him. They don't. They think he is soft and cowardly. And he is. But Loftis is determined to build a better future for his teenage son and turn the island into a tourist destination. Miraculously, he succeeds: tourists are finally coming. Unfortunately, the locals were right. After decades of calm, the old stories that seemed too ludicrous to be true start happening again.
We hope you enjoyed the three-hour ferry to New England's best-kept secret! Ignore the warnings about the fog. Everything's fine.
Cherish the historical charm of our local inn, which Mayor Loftis will prove is safe by spending a night there alone.
It's time for the annual beach opening. Lay out a towel as the mayor "tests the waters." Unrelated: Do not pick up hitchhikers.
Make sure you pack a good read for the beach! (We do not recommend self-help books on the island.)

episodes.name: What to Expect on Your Trip

info.plot: We apologize for the curfew. Please remain calm as we determine what's happening. On a separate note, keep your teenagers inside after dark.
The history of Widow's Bay is a beautiful tale of marriage, loyalty, and a love that endures beyond lifetimes. That's all.
Set out to sea with Mayor Loftis and cherished local figure, Wyck! They are attending to some business—best to simply enjoy the voyage.
Don't worry about misplaced luggage. Old baggage always resurfaces in Widow's Bay.
Please seek shelter should a centuries-old storm rage toward the island and plunge us into darkness. Enjoy a card game.

episodes.name: We Hope You Enjoyed Your Time!

info.plot: We understand that hard choices had to be made and we may never be the same, but we hope you'll visit again!

When a financial titan suddenly finds himself divorced and jobless, he starts robbing his wealthy neighbors to stay afloat. Stealing from his own social circle strangely exhilarates him—but he gradually gets tangled in a deadly web.
After losing his high-powered job, Coop attends a neighbor's barbecue, where he faces his ex-wife Mel and makes a surprising choice.
After an unsuccessful job interview, Coop pawns a stolen watch.
Nick throws a party for the neighborhood men, leading to tension between Coop and Paul. The women take a self-defense class.
An unlikely alliance begins. Nick throws Mel a surprise birthday party, where she connects with Coop.
Coop and Elena sell a stolen painting. Barney tries to hide his financial struggles from his in-laws. Coop makes a discovery at Sam's home.

episodes.name: The Things You Lost Along the Way

info.plot: After being investigated, Coop sets off on a college tour with Mel and their kids while Elena attempts to get ahold of him.
Detective Lin makes a discovery at the crime scene. Coop plays hardball with Kat. Chivo puts Elena in a tough spot.

episodes.name: When Did We Become These People?

info.plot: Coop tries getting back what he's lost. Mel and Sam clash. Coop, Nick, and Barney have a boys' night.
Coop fights for his freedom.
In season two, Andrew Cooper doubles down on his life as an unlikely suburban thief, until the arrival of a new neighbor threatens to expose his secrets and place his family at risk.
The arrival of a charming billionaire neighbor disrupts Westmont Village. Coop finds himself with an unexpected new partner in crime.
A surprise party for Tori goes awry, causing a rift in the Cooper family. Sam struggles with her alienation from the community.

episodes.name: We Were Never Supposed to Get This Old

info.plot: Ashe offers Coop a deal he can't refuse. Mel feuds with her neighbors over their new dog. Sam goes on a date.
Hari and Gretchen's Passover seder brings the neighborhood together for wine, food, and drama.
Barney deals with Ashe's audit of Nick's Strong Ass Gym. Coop and Jack travel overseas to woo a big investor. Nick throws a housewarming party.

episodes.name: For Everything Else, There Was Bowling

info.plot: Coop and his family navigate a fraught funeral service and wake.
Coop and Sam attend a wild weekend at Ashe's Hamptons estate. Mel takes things too far in her battle with the neighbors.
Mel and Tori face the consequences of their actions. Sam tries to end things with Ashe. Coop is caught between a rock and a hard place.
Alliances are tested when Coop, Barney, and Nick attend a dinner at Ashe's mansion.
In the wake of another shocking scandal, the neighborhood gathers for Father's Day at the country club.
